name: ddd-workshop
description: DDD pipeline with five paired skills covering domain learning, EventStorming 3 levels, structural decisions (ADR), per-workflow tactical plan, and orchestration installer. (1) domain-classroom — teaches general industry patterns of an unfamiliar domain in lecture mode grounded in cognitive-science 8 principles. Output: docs/learning-notes/{domain}-classroom.md. (2) eventstorming — Brandolini EventStorming 3 levels (Big Picture, Process Modeling, BC + Subdomain Discovery) unified into a single cycle tool with auto-mode-detection. One invocation reads current state and enters the appropriate cycle. Router SKILL.md with references/ submodules — only the active cycle's reference is loaded. Domain-event 4-criteria gate prevents UI/telemetry contamination. Korean natural-language + Code Identifier dual notation. Self-contained Sticky convention (10 emojis, no conflicts: 🟧Event ⭐Pivotal 🟦Command 🟪Policy 🟩Read Model 🟨Actor 🟫Aggregate 🟥External 🔥Hotspot ⬜UI). Output: docs/eventstorming/ (index.md + {NN-phase}/bigpicture.md + {NN-phase}/{workflow}.md). Closing BC + Subdomain Discovery cycle clusters 6 signals into BC candidates and updates the bigpicture appendix — ADR creation is delegated to software-design. (3) software-design — Strategic → Tactical bridge. Takes BC + Workflow candidates as input and persists structural decisions (folder structure, CQRS depth, cross-BC access, BC integration patterns, aggregate transactional boundaries, etc.) as immutable ADRs after discussing 2-4 options + trade-off matrix with the user. One invocation = one decision = one ADR. Follows Anthropic /architecture ADR format. Output: docs/adr/{NNNN}-{slug}.md. (4) tactical-design — per-workflow tactical plan generator. Reads process-modeling .md + relevant ADRs + bigpicture, and produces an in-conversation tactical plan covering Aggregate definition + invariants, Command-to-method mapping, Domain Event payload/timing, Repository interface, Read Model location, and Cross-BC reference style. Applies Vernon's Effective Aggregate Design 4 rules and reflects ADR decisions. Does not write a .md — code is the truth. Recommends pinning core invariants to module README only. One invocation = one workflow. Performs scope check before planning — if out of scope, suggests /eventstorming Cycle first. (5) playbook (#0 installer) — persists a ddd-workshop usage guide into the public rules file (AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md). The persisted section includes installation instructions (Claude Code plugin + skills.sh — for collaborators in unfamiliar-with-the-plugin environments), the skill lineup, situation-to-skill mapping table, and operating principles. Never touches Git workflow, issue tracking, PR policy, CI/CD, testing, or code style — slots into existing workflow sections, or creates a new section if none. Conflicting workflow rules surface user choices. Track A/B auto-detected. Install/Merge/Update/Conflict 4 modes. Once installed, every session auto-follows the guide via persistent context engineering.
